channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire, while key mediator Egypt accused Israel of using “starvation as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to watch
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It's almost time to see how the biggest nailbiter Oscar season of recent years concludes. Stars will converge at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood on Sunday for the 97th annual Academy Awards, which will undoubtedly see some first-time Oscar winners in top categories.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: GOP pushes ahead with citizenship voting bill. Some state election officials say it's problematic
item.description: The centerpiece election legislation from congressional Republicans would require voters to prove their citizenship when registering.
item.dc:creator: Christina A. Cassidy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Multiple wildfires in the Carolinas force evacuations, burn ban issued in South Carolina
item.description: Brush fires erupted in both North Carolina and South Carolina on Saturday, forcing officials to order evacuations as crews work to contain the fires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chanel event brings Oscar nominees together the night before the big show
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The patio of the storied Polo Lounge at the Beverly Hills Hotel was packed to the brim with Hollywood stars for the annual Chanel and Charles Finch dinner on Saturday, the final event before the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, died Saturday at the age of 92. Dukes peacefully passed away in her New York City home surrounded by family, her son, Ronald Dukes, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KRON4

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo launches political comeback with a run for New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo announced Saturday that he is running for mayor of New York City, relaunching his political career following a yearslong exile over a barrage of sexual harassment accusations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KRON4

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike and engine fire
item.description: A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the US-Mexico border
item.description: The Pentagon is sending about 3,000 more active-duty troops to the U.S.-Mexico border as President Donald Trump seeks to clamp down on illegal immigration and fulfill a central promise of his campaign, U.S. officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Grammy-nominated R&B singer Angie Stone dies in car crash
item.description: Grammy-nominated R&B singer Angie Stone, a member of the all-female hip-hop trio The Sequence and known for the hit song “Wish I Didn’t Miss You,” was killed early Saturday in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, ABC 4

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in Manhattan
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal weather and oceans agency touches people's daily lives in unnoticed ways, so massive firings there will likely cause needless deaths and a big hit to America's economy, according to the people who ran it.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: Two NYC window washers go for wild ride 78 stories up before being rescued by FDNY
item.description: Two window washers in New York City were pulled to safety 78 floors up after their scaffolding broke, sending them on a wild ride that blew them around, smashing into the building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania officer's last act after he was shot was to rescue a colleague, police chief says
item.description: A police officer who was shot to death while responding to an armed man with hostages inside a Pennsylvania hospital intensive care unit was remembered Friday as a dedicated public servant who did not hesitate to run toward danger and died a hero.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy thanks Trump for visit after Oval Office clash
item.description: The meeting escalated after Vice President JD Vance suggested a diplomatic solution could be reached with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: EPA backtracks on Trump comment about cutting staff by 65% but says major spending cuts are coming
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Despite a comment by President Donald Trump, the Environmental Protection Agency does not plan to reduce its staff by 65%, the White House and the agency said Thursday, though major budget cuts are likely.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman suspected in Colorado Tesla dealership vandalism charged in federal court
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Federal prosecutors have charged a woman in a string of vandalism against a Colorado Tesla dealership, including throwing Molotov cocktails at vehicles and spray painting “Nazi cars” on the building along with a message that appeared directed at company co-founder Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Fox 5

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, Fox 5

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Musk dominates, disparages federal workers at first Trump Cabinet meeting
item.description: Elon Musk at the first meeting of President Trump’s Cabinet defended his email demanding all federal workers report their accomplishments to his office, calling it a “pulse check” and saying anyone with a heartbeat and neurons could complete it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Judge dismisses racketeering indictment against New Jersey Democratic power broker, co-defendants
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A state judge on Wednesday agreed to toss out racketeering charges against New Jersey Democratic power broker George Norcross and those charged alongside him.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4
